issue_title: MagicBot StateMachine breaks when its last state is timed and has a default state
issue_body:
In the example below I would expect after b is done the state machine would transition to either the **default** or **a** state. Instead the console shows the state machine switching back and forth between the **default** and **a** state (it never transitions from **a** to **b**). If you run this code in the simulator with teleop enabled the console shows this:

> 19:36:23:486 WARNING : robot               : Default MagicRobot.disabledPeriodic() method... Overload me!
19:36:23:642 INFO    : nt                  : CONNECTED 127.0.0.1 port 51670 (pynetworktables 2017.0.8)
19:36:23:685 WARNING : pyfrc.sim.ui        : pygame not detected, real joystick support not loaded
a
b
default
a
default
a
default
a
default
a
default

Calling the StateMachine done method seems to fix this issue, maybe it should be called automatically at the end of the final state?

Here's the code:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3

import wpilib
from magicbot import StateMachine, state, timed_state, MagicRobot
from magicbot.state_machine import default_state

class SM(StateMachine):

    @default_state
    def d(self):
        print('default')

    @state(first=True)
    def a(self):
        print('a')
        self.next_state('b')

    @timed_state(duration=.01)
    def b(self):
        print('b')


class MyRobot(MagicRobot):

    sm = SM

    def createObjects(self):
        pass

    def teleopPeriodic(self):
        self.sm.engage()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    wpilib.run(MyRobot)


```

issue_title: pipenv update fails, requiring setuptools
Pipenv install hangs, simply by adding private repository
issue_body:
I've recently added a private repository to Pipfile.

```
[[source]]
url = "https://pypi.python.org/simple"
verify_ssl = true
name = "pypi"

[[source]]
url = "https://pypi.****.io/${AUTH}/***/"
verify_ssl = true
name = "mypypi"

[packages]
...
```
It's all I've done. I did not add any private packages. Just setting the private repo.

##### Issue description

After adding the private repository, the following command
```
pipenv install --dev
```
hangs in CI environment (Appveyor). The install process timeouts after 1 hour. Without adding the private source, it takes a couple of minutes.
It does not hang in Docker-based CI, nor on my Windows 10 machine.

##### Expected result
I expect it not to hang.

##### Actual result

```

$ "C:\\***\\pip.exe" install   --verbose  --no-deps  -r "C:\\***\\1\\pipenv-x1ion6s4-requirements\\pipenv-hja6lxq6-requirement.txt" --require-hashes -i https://pypi.python.org/simple --extra-index-url https://pypi.***.io/***/***/ --exists-action w
Installing 'networkx==2.1 --hash=sha256:64272ca418972b70a196cb15d9c85a5a6041f09a2f32e0d30c0255f25d458bb1'
$ "C:\\***\\pip.exe" install   --verbose  --no-deps  -r "C:\\***\\1\\pipenv-x1ion6s4-requirements\\pipenv-s7weu7a0-requirement.txt" --require-hashes -i https://pypi.python.org/simple --extra-index-url https://pypi.***.io/***/***/ --exists-action w
Installing 'nose==1.3.7 --hash=sha256:9ff7c6cc443f8c51994b34a667bbcf45afd6d945be7477b52e97516fd17c53ac --hash=sha256:dadcddc0aefbf99eea214e0f1232b94f2fa9bd98fa8353711dacb112bfcbbb2a --hash=sha256:f1bffef9cbc82628f6e7d7b40d7e255aefaa1adb6a1b1d26c69a8b79e6208a98'
$ "C:\\***\\pip.exe" install   --verbose  --no-deps  -r "C:\\***\\1\\pipenv-x1ion6s4-requirements\\pipenv-jqcm4anq-requirement.txt" --require-hashes -i https://pypi.python.org/simple --extra-index-url https://pypi.***.io/***/***/ --exists-action w
```

It just freezes at this one last line without explanation. Is there a way to know which package it's currently trying to install ?

My tools are up-to-date (Python 3.6.6 64 bits, pip 18.0, Pipenv 2018.7.1)

```
python -V
Python 3.6.6
pip -V
pip 18.0 from c:\python36-x64\lib\site-packages\pip (python 3.6)
pip install pipenv
Collecting pipenv
  Downloading https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/eb/64/9b2747d54f2008ac3dfe86c0b1c8ec126042726fd8a540d5208d26732701/pipenv-2018.7.1-py3-none-any.whl (5.0MB)
```
##### Steps to replicate

1. Add a private repository to a Pipfile
2. Add some official Pypi dependencies
3. Run `pipenv install --dev` inside appveyor CI

The problem does not seem due to Appveyor, because I have configured another repository with Appveyor + pipenv + private repository without issue.

- Any idea of what is going on here ?
- Any idea of how I can get additional information for debugging ?
- BTW, why is pipenv passing `--extra-index-url` flag to pip all the time ? Is it not considering that all package that don't specify a particular repository inside the Pipfile should be hosted on canonical pypi, therefore not needing the flag ?

Thank you for your help, let me know if there's something I can do to help you best.

issue_title: linger_ms = 0 not working with rdlibkafka
issue_body:
Hi,
I`m using pykafka and it`s been great, but I found a tiny bug:
when using rdlibkafka, and trying to create a producer with linger_ms=0, it raises this exception:

RdKafkaException(\'Configuration property "queue.buffering.max.ms" value 0 is outside allowed range 1..900000\\n\',)

However, in the documentation , it says that "linger_ms=0 indicates no lingering."
So I guess it should notice that this option does not work with rdlibkafka

Thanks for this awesome package!
Amit :)

**PyKafka version**: 2.4.0
**Kafka version**: 0.9.1

issue_title: ValidationFailedError: <unprintable ValidationFailedError object> on duplicate bank_account
issue_body:
Trying to create the same bank account twice with `client.customer_bank_accounts.create` raises an unprintable ValidationFailedError:

```
        error = response.json()['error']
        exception_class = errors.ApiError.exception_for(error['type'])
>       raise exception_class(error)
E       ValidationFailedError: <unprintable ValidationFailedError object>
```

Looking at `errors.py` it seems that it wants to format a `field` key in `__str__` which is not returned by the API. The actual error JSON object is:

```

issue_title: SMTPMailer does not reconnect to SMTP server
issue_body:
```zope.sendmail.mailer.SMTPMailer``` does not reestablish an SMTP connection after sending the first mail.

```SMTPMailer``` in previous versions of zope.sendmail (up until 3.7.5) would always establish an new SMTP connection when ```send``` was being called. Its very own ```QueueProcessorThread``` and other projects may rely on the fact that one ```SMTPMailer``` instance can be reused (immediately or after some time) to send multiple mails.
However, since 4.0.0 the object caches the ```self.connection``` (an  ```SMTP``` instance). At the end of ```send()``` it will call ```self.connection.quit()``` and/or ```self.connection.close()```, making the connection unusable. Similar code is in ```abort()```. In all of these cases, ```self.connection``` should be set to ```None```, or the usage of ```self.connection``` should be reverted to a local variable ```connection``` that is returned by ```self.vote()```.

issue_title: `pip install kivy` fails with `'../include/config.pxi' not found`
issue_body:
I am attempting to install kivy in a virtualenv, following [the official instructions](https://kivy.org/docs/installation/installation-linux.html#installation-in-a-virtual-environment). However, when I run `pip install kivy` I get the following error (full traceback and steps to reproduce below). I am running Ubuntu 16.04 LTS 'Xenial'. **Where should config.pxi come from, and what should it contain? Am I missing an installation step, or is this due to an assumption in Kivy's setup.py?**


       [...]
       File "/home/vagrant/.virtualenvs/kivy/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/Cython/Build/Dependencies.py", line 474, in included_files
         include_path = self.context.find_include_file(include, None)
       File "/home/vagrant/.virtualenvs/kivy/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/Cython/Compiler/Main.py", line 274, in find_include_file
         error(pos, "'%s' not found" % filename)
       File "/home/vagrant/.virtualenvs/kivy/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/Cython/Compiler/Errors.py", line 177, in error
         raise InternalError(message)
     Cython.Compiler.Errors.InternalError: Internal compiler error: '../include/config.pxi' not found

The installation process looks for config.pxi in these locations:

* /tmp/pip-build-MB2bZR/include/config.pxi
* /home/vagrant/.virtualenvs/kivy/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/Cython/include/config.pxi

## Investigation

### The problem is consistent across versions

The problem occurs both with `pip install kivy==1.10.0` and with `pip install git+https://github.com/kivy/kivy.git@master`.

The problem occurs in [`find_include_file` in Cython 0.25's Main.py](https://github.com/cython/cython/blob/0.25/Cython/Compiler/Main.py#L268).

Because the steps to reproduce include `sudo apt-get install`, I have placed
them in a Vagrantfile. Vagrant makes it trivial to create and destroy virtual
machines that have the same environment every time. This is useful for
reproducible development environments, but also to demonstrate bugs.

If you do not want to use Vagrant, look for the code below.

      config.vm.provision "shell", inline: <<-SHELL

      SHELL

### Vagrant usage

* Install Vagrant from [https://www.vagrantup.com]()
* Create a directory, and place the contents below in a file called `Vagrantfile`
* `cd` to the directory and run `vagrant up`
* The problem mentioned above will occur at the end of the provisioning.
* You can use `vagrant ssh` to SSH into the virtual machine
* You can use `vagrant destroy` + `vagrant up` to recreate the machine from scratch

### Vagrantfile

Call this file `Vagrantfile`. See the previous section for usage.

    # -*- mode: ruby -*-

          unzip \

        . /usr/share/virtualenvwrapper/virtualenvwrapper.sh
        mkvirtualenv kivy
        kivypip=$HOME/.virtualenvs/kivy/bin/pip

        $kivypip install cython==0.25

issue_title: Use travis to automatically test builds on different platforms
2.6.0 about 25% slower than 2.5.2 with MKL
issue_body:
I performed some benchmark comparisons using the WinPython distribution  at https://winpython.github.io/
Like Anaconda on Windows, WinPython uses numexpr and numpy compiled with MKL from wheels on this web site: www.lfd.uci.edu/~gohlke/pythonlibs/

I used the following benchmark script on my Haswell Core i7-4700HQ-based Windows 8.1 PC, which is dominated by numexpr and representative of the way I typically use numexpr:
```
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import numpy as np
import numexpr as ne
from timeit import default_timer as timer

x = np.ones(100000)
scaler = -1J
start = timer()
for k in range(10000):
    cexp = ne.evaluate('exp(scaler * x)')
exec_time=(timer() - start)
print
print("Execution took", str(round(exec_time, 3)), "seconds")
```
I got the same performance for Python 3.4 and 3.5 environments.  With numexpr 2.5.1 and 2.5.2, I got 7.3 seconds.  With numexpr 2.6.0, I got 9.1 seconds, which 25% slower.

My test environment can be reproduced from the following distribution installers:
numexpr 2.5.1 from WinPython-64bit-3.4.4.2.exe at https://sourceforge.net/projects/winpython/files/WinPython_3.4/3.4.4.2/
numexpr 2.6.0 from WinPython-64bit-3.4.4.3rc.exe at https://sourceforge.net/projects/winpython/files/WinPython_3.4/3.4.4.3/betas/

The list of changed packages is available here:
https://github.com/winpython/winpython/issues/309

issue_title: Detecting if is_authenticated is a method causes deprecation warnings
issue_body:
Note the implementation you made for the client [here](https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/d8d3c834b7d3792bdef93a55e876ace1630579c6/raven/contrib/django/client.py#L150)

This is a problem because it will cause a deprecation warning every time the module runs a logger. 

I have a special case where I have to manage a large website. I use raven to send logging events when it detects a deprecation warning, this will cause it to send another deprecation warning event from the module itself.
![](http://i.imgur.com/drrKVYt.png)

Since technically in Django 1.11, `is_authenticated()` is still valid until it is fully phased out in 2.0.

Wouldn't a check for this be more effected like:
```python
            if hasattr(user, 'is_authenticated'):
                authenticated = user.is_authenticated
            elif callable(user.is_authenticated):
                # Django < 1.11
                authenticated = user.is_authenticated()
            elif not authenticated:
                return None
```

issue_title: Groupby can be sped up
issue_body:
When performing a simple groupby with logging turned on, the following timings are observed:
```
>>> y = x[:, {"v1": sum(f.v1)}, f.id1]
    call DataTable.rowindex
    done DataTable.rowindex in 0.001 ms
    call DataTable.rowindex
    done DataTable.rowindex in 0.000 ms
    call DataTable.sort(...)
    done DataTable.sort(...) in 221.671 ms
  start colsnode....
    call CLSNAME.expr_column(...)
    done CLSNAME.expr_column(...) in 76.100 ms
    call CLSNAME.expr_reduceop(...)
    done CLSNAME.expr_reduceop(...) in 0.052 ms
    call del Column
    done del Column in 0.004 ms
    call CLSNAME.expr_column(...)
    done CLSNAME.expr_column(...) in 172.068 ms
    call CLSNAME.expr_reduceop(...)
    done CLSNAME.expr_reduceop(...) in 7.872 ms
    call del Column
    done del Column in 0.017 ms
    call ColumnSet.columns_from_columns(...)
    done ColumnSet.columns_from_columns(...) in 0.003 ms
    call del Column
    done del Column in 0.001 ms
    call del Column
    done del Column in 0.000 ms
  end colsnode....
    call ColumnSet.to_datatable(...)
    done ColumnSet.to_datatable(...) in 0.006 ms
    call DataTable.nrows
    done DataTable.nrows in 0.003 ms
    call DataTable.ncols
    done DataTable.ncols in 0.000 ms
    call DataTable.nrows
    done DataTable.nrows in 0.000 ms
  Preparation time:         5.1939487 ms
  Time to compute rowsnode: 0.0612736 ms
  Time to compute groupby:  221.7738628 ms
  Time to compute colsnode: 258.8489056 ms
  Time to make res Frame:   0.6322861 ms
  Total time:               486.5102768 ms
    call del ColumnSet
    done del ColumnSet in 0.000 ms
    call del Groupby
    done del Groupby in 0.002 ms
    call del RowIndex
    done del RowIndex in 3.606 ms
    call del DataTable
    done del DataTable in 0.640 ms 
```

For comparison, the same operation in R data.table:
```
>  DT[, sum(v1), keyby=id1, verbose=T] -> ans
Detected that j uses these columns: v1 
Finding groups using forderv ... 0.091sec 
Finding group sizes from the positions (can be avoided to save RAM) ... 0.000sec 
lapply optimization is on, j unchanged as 'sum(v1)'
GForce optimized j to 'gsum(v1)'
Making each group and running j (GForce TRUE) ... 0.103sec 
```

There are 2 significant points here:
* sort takes much longer
* expr_column() operations take too long, they should be merged with the subsequent reduce operations

issue_title: Icon request: font-envelope-flat
issue_body:
Hello, Awesomeness!

The two most common types of envelopes are the shape of the #9/#10 envelope for folded letter paper (already well represented in fa) and the "flat" envelope for which folding is not needed with letter size paper.  Flats hold more pages, so they're typically used for larger documents or for documents (like pictures) which cannot be bent and need room for "stiffeners".

**Use Cases**
I will be using it to represent larger documents or pictures that I have mailed in that type of envelope.  

**Image**
Enclosed is a rough draft for a flat envelope icon.  A flat typically has its opening on the short side and measures 9"x12", so a 3:4 aspect ratio would increase it's recognizability.

![flat envelope icon draft](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5002574/24707587/351c91a6-19e2-11e7-9e5b-c4a863ddd31c.jpg)

issue_title: credentials support with privilege escalation *

issue_title: default perms on ~/.tower_cli.cfg
issue_body: By default ~/.tower_cli.cfg is created in a manner that relies on system umask.  For any other file, I'd say that was reasonable, but as this will have a plaintext password in it, I'd advocate for the more restrictive 0600 permissions.

issue_title: Documentation - relative routing with router controller
issue_body: The endpoint routing section incorrectly states that the `x-router-controller` property may be set for an operation to make the operationId property relative. However, the correct property name is `x-swagger-router-controller` (see [connexion.resovler:60](https://github.com/zalando/connexion/blob/master/connexion/resolver.py#L60)).

issue_title: GroupPicker fields cannot be read out like other fields.
issue_body:
I have a project where I have added a GroupPicker field to an issue. When I try to read the issue, I cannot (easily) access he name of the group listed:

```python
issue = jira.issue('SIS-11', fields = ['customfield_10800'])
print('issue', issue)

print('grouplist', issue.fields.customfield_10800)
print('group', issue.fields.customfield_10800[0])
print('group-name', issue.fields.customfield_10800[0]._session['name'])

print('expected', issue.fields.customfield_10800[0].name)  # Boom!
```

yields (lines reordered):

```
issue SIS-11
grouplist [<JIRA Resource at 4402162936>]
group <JIRA Resource at 4402162936>
group-name jira-software-users
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "jira-python/jira/resources.py", line 160, in __getattr__
    return self[item]
TypeError: 'Resource' object is not subscriptable

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "jira-python/sample.py", line __, in <module>
    print('expected', issue.fields.customfield_10800[0].name)  # Boom!
  File "jira-python/jira/resources.py", line 173, in __getattr__
    if hasattr(self, 'raw') and item in self.raw:
TypeError: argument of type 'NoneType' is not iterable
```

The response of the REST call looks quite sensible:

```
GET http://jira:8080/rest/api/2/issue/SIS-11?os_authType=basic&fields=customfield_10800

{
  "expand": "renderedFields,names,schema,operations,editmeta,changelog,versionedRepresentations",
  "id": "10600",
  "self": "http://jira:8080/rest/api/2/issue/10600",
  "key": "SIS-11",
  "fields": {
    "customfield_10800": [
      {
        "name": "jira-software-users",
        "self": "http://jira:8080/rest/api/2/group?groupname=jira-software-users"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

As far as I can see from debugging, the problem lies in creating a new object upon encountering 'self' (good), but then not adding the key/value pairs on the same level as properties:

https://github.com/pycontribs/jira/blob/0243eac8570366170af98789e38c1a55c1c6eda3/jira/resources.py#L919-L928

Would that be the proper way to handle this?

issue_title: Maximal and minimal bounds should be taken from model (not set to arbitrary values)
issue_body:
#### Problem description
Many tests of memote rely on arbitrary upper and lower bounds of (-1000, 1000). Example are for instance in `memote/suite/tests/test_basic.py`

```
def find_nonzero_constrained_reactions(model):
    """Return list of reactions with non-zero, non-maximal bounds."""
    return [rxn for rxn in model.reactions if
            0 > rxn.lower_bound > -1000 or
            0 < rxn.upper_bound < 1000]

def find_unconstrained_reactions(model):
    """Return list of reactions that are not constrained at all."""
    return [rxn for rxn in model.reactions if
            rxn.lower_bound <= -1000 and
            rxn.upper_bound >= 1000]
```
The actual maximal bounds (or default bounds) from the given model must be taken for all these tests.
If I have a model with (-900, 900) max bounds most reactions will be incorrectly classified as non-maximal bounds.

issue_title: Do full pass over RST docs and docstrings after datastore API revamp is completed
issue_body:
```Python
from gcloud import datastore
dataset = datastore.get_dataset('dataset-id-here')
# Then do other things...
query = dataset.query().kind('EntityKind')
entity = dataset.entity('EntityKind')
```
This doesn't work, and is in both README and official docs. It is documented in ```help(datastore.get_dataset)``` but not everyone may be aware.
```Python
>>> from gcloud import datastore
>>> dataset = datastore.get_dataset('dataset-id', email, key_path)
>>> # Now you can do things with the dataset.
>>> dataset.query().kind('TestKind').fetch()
```
This also makes the envorinment variables optional I think, and not mandatory as per docs. It should also be documented that ```key_path``` expects the raw p12 key and not the JSON version, unlike the environment variable.

https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/gcloud-python/datastore-api.html#cloud-datastore-in-10-seconds

  File "/mnt/nfs2/inf/thk22/.virtualenvs/nlp/lib/python3.4/site-packages/dill/dill.py", line 729, in _import_module
    return __import__(import_name)
TypeError: __import__() argument 1 must be str, not dict
```

I traced down this error a little bit and the problematic line is: ` elif '__name__' in obj and obj != _main_module.__dict__ and obj is getattr(_import_module(obj['__name__'],True), '__dict__', None):` (line 822-823 in dill.py, line numbers are different in the crashlog because i've added a bunch of log statements throughout the file for debugging), the actual crash happens in the fn `_import_module()`. However the problem is that `__name__` happens to be a key in my `dict` and thus the wrong branch is executed in `save_module_dict()` with `_import_module()` not receiving the `str` it expects (but a `dict`). I can't really change the name of the key or remove it (at least not at that processing stage) as the `dict` contains word counts over a large NLP corpus.

I am well aware that this issue is probably an absolute border case, however I would be grateful for some suggestions as to how I can workaround the problem in my local `dill` copy without breaking any of the functionality.

issue_title: Build graph construction failed encountered over and over
issue_body:
Running pants 1.7.0rc0.  We've had several reports of users getting into a state where pants complains that a target doesn't exist in a BUILD file.  Repeat the build and pants complains about a different target in a different BUILD file.  Keep doing this over and over again and you keep getting the error from different BUILD files.  The files look fine and are unchanged compared to git.  Running clean-all, deleting `~/.cache/pants/lmdb_store`, deleting `.cache` don't clear up this problem.  The only work around we've found, which sounds crazy, is to delete the source files and check them out from git again, even though there were no changes to the files.

The errors we get are like this:

```
Exception caught: (<class 'pants.build_graph.address_lookup_error.AddressLookupError'>)

Exception message: Build graph construction failed: ResolveError "utils" was not found in namespace "horizon/java/com/r9/horizon/common/compareto/slide/utils". Did you mean one of:
```

It will print this when running `./pants compile` or `./pants list` with a target.  Opening the file it looks normal and has one unnamed target.  Run the same command again and the `Build graph construction failed` error happens again, but this time in a different directory.  They always seem to fall into the same top level directory (in the example above, the `horizon` directory).

We tried the following:
```
rm -r .pants.d
rm -r .cache
rm -r ~/.cache/lmdb_store
```
The errors continue.

To clear this up we did the following:
```
rm -r horizon
git checkout -- horizon
```
This replaces the files with new copies, but they would be identical in content, since there were no changes relative to git in the source tree.

I don't know how to get the build into this state, but I tried to poke around on someone's laptop who ran into this problem.  I couldn't figure out how to recover besides checking out from git again.

issue_title: Setup-py task does not resolve symlink
issue_body:
There seems to be a small mismatch in the behavior of `./pants setup-py` and `./pants binary` regarding the resolution of symbolic links.

Given the following code, where `.auroraversion` is pointing to an external file, the `binary` task resolves the symlink and packages the original file. 
```
python_library(
  name = 'auroraversion',
  resources = [ '.auroraversion' ]
)
```
However, the `setup-py` task is only packaging the symlink itself. This leads to an sdist with a dangling symlink which cannot be converted to a wheel.

Expected behavior would be that the `setup-py` task behaves `binary` here and include the original file.

issue_title: Custom panic handler wipes out backtrace rendering
issue_body:
Inserting a panic in the native engine on master, you will see:
```
$ ./pants list 3rdparty/:
CRITICAL] panic at 'not yet implemented', src/rust/engine/src/scheduler.rs:40
CRITICAL] Please file a bug at https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/issues.
fatal runtime error: failed to initiate panic, error 5
Fatal Python error: Aborted
```

...due to the custom panic handler installed by https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/7319b21030f020ffefabebda30e5f6fbf48cf248/src/python/pants/engine/native.py#L721

But, annoyingly, this custom panic handler disables the highly useful `RUST_BACKTRACE` facility. Disabling the panic handler by commenting out its installation, I see:
```
thread '<unnamed>' panicked at 'not yet implemented', src/rust/engine/src/scheduler.rs:40
note: Run with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` for a backtrace.
fatal runtime error: failed to initiate panic, error 5
Fatal Python error: Aborted
```
... and when I pass `RUST_BACKTRACE=1`, I get an actual useful backtrace pointing to the location of the panic.

issue_title: TypeError: environment can only contain strings with Python 3 on Windows
issue_body:
Hi,

First of all, I'm very glad to see this project still live! Keep up the good work. 🥇 

I have an issue to report but luckily also some solutions for it.

## Issue:

Triggering CodeIntel results in an error that I found in `codeintel.log`:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  [...]
  File "site-packages\codeintel\codeintel2\lang_php.py", line 980, in _php_info_from_php
    p = process.ProcessOpen(argv, env=env.get_all_envvars())
  File "site-packages\codeintel\process.py", line 581, in __init__
    creationflags=flags)
  File "subprocess.py", line 709, in __init__
    restore_signals, start_new_session)
  File "site-packages\codeintel\process.py", line 399, in _execute_child
    startupinfo)

TypeError: environment can only contain strings
```

My environment:

* Windows 10 64-bit
* Python 3.6.3 64-bit, tried also on Python 3.5 32-bit
* Sublime Text Build 3153 from Dev channel

## Solution

The culprit is this bit of code:

https://github.com/SublimeCodeIntel/CodeIntel/blob/296df71659d38cddb9f47dcd6e517cbebf9ba043/codeintel/process.py#L499-L508

While it is necessary for Python 2, it is breaking in 3 because all keys and values end up being encoded as `bytes`.

I'm not sure what's the best approach here, I came up with:

1. Add `if six.PY2` at line 499
2. Check if `key`/`value` is not `isinstance(value, string_types)` and encode only then.

Similar has been done in https://github.com/django/django/commit/fc6b90bdb7a9531e988245942f79518308616b7b.

issue_title: Problem found in DataItemBits::getBits(.....)
Memento endpoint no longer resolves embedded resources
issue_body: `ipwb index ipwb/samples/warcs/froggie.warc.gz | ipwb replay` then access `http://127.0.0.1:5000/20170301192639/cs.odu.edu/~mkelly/semester/2017_spring/remotefroggie.html` in a browser--the image is broken. The logic in `/memento/` endpoint no longer resolves requests for the resource at the nearest datetime, thus the image that should be resolved from http://127.0.0.1:5000/memento/http://matkelly.com/froggies/frog.png as rerouted by the service worker never resolves.

issue_title: Show availability of memento in ipwb replay homepage
issue_body:
URIs listed on the main page of the replay system based on a provided CDXJ may or may not be available based on a variety of reasons (no copy in ipfs, no local network connectivity, etc). The tasks in this ticket is two-fold:

1. Surface the IPFS hash of the root memento, associate it with data-* attribute on the `li`. The hash is already available in the scraped cdxj.
2. Display the status of the URI-M through some (e.g., colored) indicator adjacent to the link OR convey it through the link in some other way.

I believe a library/module already exists to check the status of an IPFS hash w/o needing a local IPFS node. Perhaps this is part of js-ipfs now.

issue_title: Several properties / functions use enums as constants
issue_body:
For example, property `powerline_freq` uses enum `PowerlineFrequency` which actually contains constants for 50.0 and 60.0. These aren't actually enumerated values and arguably make the API harder to use because there are more types to know about.

* These were inherited from CVI APIs.
* In many cases in LabVIEW we don't use enums for the equivalent properties.
* In C, we do often provide these values as constants, but not enums.

issue_title: Behavior of PitchClassDistributionFeature doesn’t match documentation
issue_body:
The documentation for the jSymbolic class `PitchClassDistributionFeature` reads as follows:

> A feature array with 12 entries where the first holds the frequency of the bin of the pitch class histogram with the highest frequency, and the following entries holding the successive bins of the histogram, wrapping around if necessary.

This is similar to the original jSymbolic implementation. However, the actual music21 implementation doesn't reorder the array to put the highest-frequency. Furthermore, the music21 implementation normalizes the highest frequency to 1 and expresses the frequencies of the other pitch classes as fractions of that.

(I do not know which other classes, if any, have an implementation that varies from the documentation.)

issue_title: Handling RGB 3D confocal time series image stacks
issue_body:
I'm working with a collection of 3D images using `ImageSequenceND`, but I'm running into numerous problems related to the RGB format of the images.

I read the images using the arguments from the tutorial:

```
frames = pims.ImageSequenceND("010/*.tif", axes_identifiers = ["z", "t"])
frames.bundle_axes = ["z", "y", "x"]
frames.iter_axis = "t"
print(frames)
```

And I get what is more or less expected:

```
<ImageSequenceND>
Source: /data/3d/010/*.tif
Axes: 5
Axis 'z' size: 41
Axis 't' size: 62
Axis 'y' size: 500
Axis 'x' size: 502
Axis 'c' size: 3
Pixel Datatype: uint8
```

However, when I run `print(frames[0].shape)`, I only get `(41, 500, 502)`. What happened to the color channels?

Whenever I attempt to use the images in downstream analysis, they seem only to be using the first color axis (e.g., using `trackpy.locate`).

When I attempt to instead convert the images to grayscale, it seems like the `ImageSequenceND` constructor ignores the `process_func` argument entirely that would allow the images to be converted; implementing a grayscale conversion or simply passing in `skimage.color.rgb2gray` has no effect on the image sequence.

I could write an entire subclass that is identical to `ImageSequenceND` but simply flattens the images it reads in, but that seems excessive. Any suggestions?

>     import socket

* New applications will reflect the ENV variable.
* ENV variable can be overridden if set at the application level.

### Nice to haves:
Aliasing. It would be great if I could set `SOME_VAR=$OTHER_VAR`. This would allow me to do `NEW_RELIC_APP_NAME=$DEIS_APP`.

issue_title: Oddly, example-ruby-sinatra runs slow with k8s as scheduler
issue_body:
I'm having a tough time getting to the bottom of this.  Opening the issue just to ensure it's tracked.

I've discovered, by chance, that when I deploy the example-ruby-sinatra app using k8s as the scheduler, responses from the application are very slow.  Oddly this does not happen with Fleet as the scheduler _and_ it does not happen with other apps.

Digging in farther, if you `nse` to a container running the sinatra app and `curl -L http://localhost:5000`, it responds very quickly.  If however, you make the request using the IP on the overlay network that Docker has assigned to the container _or_ if you use the IP that k8s uses for the service, then the response takes 10+ seconds.

Compare this to when Fleet is used as the scheduler and requests made using the IP on the overlay network that Docker has assigned to the container are very fast.

Something is fishy and I am still on the hunt.

issue_title: Fix test in test_vmc_handler.py
issue_body:
### What is wrong?
The expected `TransactionFailed` error in here in `test_vmc_handler.py` is not caused by expected case.
```python
# if a header is added before its parent header is added, `add_header` should fail
    # TransactionFailed raised when assertions fail
    with pytest.raises(TransactionFailed):
        header_parent_not_added = mk_testing_colhdr(
            vmc,
            shard_id,
            header0_1.hash,
            1,
        )
        add_header_constant_call(vmc, header_parent_not_added)
```

After some debugging, it turns out that this error is caused by wrong period number provided, instead of the expected cause(`if a header is added before its parent header is added, `add_header` should fail`).
Cause: Since a local function call(i.e., no real transaction broadcasted) in web3.py will use the latest mined block number as `block.number`, this cause the `addHeader` to fetch the wrong block hash with the lates mined block number(should be latest mined block number+1) and results in assertion failure in contract.

### How can it be fixed

Check if we can customize environment parameters(block number) in web3.py or come up with different cases that would have the expected result(`if a header is added before its parent header is added, `add_header` should fail`).

issue_title: Validate genesis header
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Right now, we aren't doing anything to validate that the genesis header we download is compatible with the genesis setup used in `trinity`. (not that we support arbitrary genesis settings now, but we will at some point)

### How can it be fixed

See some discussion at: https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/pull/883#discussion_r194305867

Evaluate the genesis file setup we'll use (I think an EIP is in draft or merged on this). Does it fully specify the header? If so, force header creation before launching p2p. If not, save the genesis fields for later validation.

issue_title: Invoke ambiguous contract functions
Add a "Common Problems" page to the documentation
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

There are certain issues that are encountered regularly by users.  


### How can it be fixed?

We should add a "Common Problems" page to the documentation which tries to capture these succinctly and provides answers/solutions.

- using `web3.eth.sendTransaction` when connected to infura
- trying to access `web3.personal` API when using parity or when the `personal` API is not enabled with geth.
- trying to call methods off of the `Web3` class rather than an instance of the class.
- improper instantiation of contract instances like trying to call functions off of the contract class.

issue_title: Problem with Django 1.7 migrations?
issue_body:
I just ran my first migration on a model that has an hstore field in it.  I had changed the default of a datetime field within the hstore field (because of https://github.com/tomchristie/django-rest-framework/issues/2687) and the migration blew up with:
```
ValueError: Cannot alter field batches.Collection.end_date into app.MyModel.end_date - they do not properly define db_type (are you using PostGIS 1.5 or badly-written custom fields?)
```
So I took a look at what the migration was doing:
```python
migrations.AlterField(
            model_name='mymodel',
            name='end_date',
            field=django_hstore.virtual.VirtualField(default=datetime.date(2015, 1, 1)),
        )
```
It seems like the django_hstore.virtual.VirtualField fields shouldn't be included in the migration.  I wanted to check and see if others had this issue or if I was just missing something before I looked in to how we might avoid this (other than manually editing the migration every time)

issue_title: `vs2015_runtime` platform-dependent universal C-runtime version
issue_body:
As of the version 14 of Visual Studio (2015), the C-runtime dlls were separated into two parts.

- the *Universal C-Runtime* libraries `api-ms-win-*`, which are dependent on the version of windows, but should not depend on the version of Visual Studio from MSVC 14 onward (future versions).
    These `api-ms-win-*` dll are present by default in windows 10, and available as an update for earlier versions of windows. (see [here](https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/kb/2999226).)

- the compiler version-dependent part of the msvc runtime that is provided with the redistributable package.

Since anaconda must be installable without admin rights, it cannot require the universal C runtime system update for earlier versions of windows than windows 10. What is done instead is that the `vs2015_runtime` package comes bundled with *a* version of those dlls, apparently the windows 7 versions.

It is not going to be a problem on windows 10 because the system libraries are searched *before* the `Library` subdirectory the of Anaconda install.

However, on other versions of windows (earlier than windows 10, different from windows 7), this version of the C-runtime is probably not compatible with the system.

issue_title: graphviz package doesn't add executable to PATH on windows
issue_body:
The graphviz dot executable is installed to ``<anaconda>/Library/bin/graphviz/`` (I think, don't have a machine here to double check - but there's definitely a graphviz sub-folder).
But this folder is not added to the executable search path. That means that it can not be used basically, and using the python graphviz package fails.
I just manually fixed this issue at around 10 laptops in my workshop :-/
doing ``!set PATH=PATH;C:\path\to\anaconda\Library\bin\graphviz\`` fixed it.

issue_title: noarch for Python 2 or 3 only
issue_body: Would be great if it were possible to have `noarch` packages that are Python 2 only or Python 3 only. Some Google Python libraries will likely remain Python 2 only and it would be nice to still have a `noarch` option for them. Also some of the core SciPy stack is moving to Python 3 only and it would be nice to still have a `noarch` option for them as well.

issue_title: Cannot clear scroll over multiple indices
issue_body:
**Elasticsearch version**: 5.0 (master)
**Elasticsearch Python version**: 2.3.0

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:

When creating multiple indices (25 in the reproduction scenario) and doing a scroll search over the index, it is not possible to close it properly. 

**Steps to reproduce**:
 1. Start a fresh single-node Elasticsearch cluster
 2. Run the following script (requires Python3):

```python
import elasticsearch

es = elasticsearch.Elasticsearch()


doc = {
    "vendor": "Elastic",
    "package": "Elasticsearch",
    "version": "5.0.0"
}

num_indices = 25
print("Creating [%d] indices" % num_indices)

# create a few indices and add a document
for i in range(1, num_indices):
    index_name = "scroll-test-%d" % i
    es.indices.create(index=index_name, ignore=400)
    es.index(index=index_name, doc_type="packages", body=doc)


print("Performing a scroll search")

r = es.search(index="scroll-test-*", doc_type="packages", sort="_doc", scroll="10s", size=5)
scroll_id = r["_scroll_id"]
print("scroll_id length = [%d] characters" % len(scroll_id))

for page in range(5):
    hit_count = len(r["hits"]["hits"])
    print("Retrieving results for page [%d] ([%d] results)" % (page, hit_count))
    if hit_count == 0:
        # done
        break
    r = es.scroll(scroll_id=scroll_id, scroll="10s")

print("Clearing scroll")
es.clear_scroll(scroll_id=scroll_id)
```

**Provide logs (if relevant)**:

Server logs:

```

This indicates that the Python client provides the scroll id in the URL but the [Elasticsearch documentation](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/search-request-scroll.html#_clear_scroll_api) says that the scroll id has to be provided in the body when using the clear scroll API:

So the fix seems to be to put the scroll id in the body instead of the URL.

issue_title: data lake cli (dls) upload / download chunk and block size support
issue_body:
### Description
The default chunk size (268MB) prevents parallelization of smaller transfers.
Exposing additional parameters (chunk, buffer, block size) in the CLI would allow to take advantage of parallel transfers for smaller files.

---

```



- name: --chunk-size
  | type: int
  | short-summary: 'Number of bytes for a chunk. Large files are split into chunks. Files smaller than this number will always be transferred in a single thread. Default 268435456'
  | - name: --buffer-size
  | type: int
  | short-summary: 'Number of bytes for internal buffer. This block cannot be bigger than a chunk and cannot be smaller than a block. Default 4194304'
  | - name: --block-size
  | type: int
  | short-summary: 'Number of bytes for a block. Within each chunk, we write a smaller block for each API call. This block cannot be bigger than a chunk. Default:4194304'
 
```

issue_title: SDK currently does not address the scenario where a large file needs to be downloaded serially to a non-seekable stream
issue_body:
### Which service(blob, file, queue) does this issue concern?
blob/file

### What problem was encountered?
referencing with some edits: https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/issues/6608:

Currently the `max_connections=1` arguments for downloads will only attempt to pull the larger files in one piece. When the file it is trying to download is larger than the memory on the workstation running the task the process consumes the workstation's entire memory and swap file then fails.

Looking to get the download methods to treat the `max_connections=1` like it does when the max connections is set to 2 or more. That is to handle files larger than the memory of the workstation by breaking the file into multiple peices and make multiple requests to get the full file to download.

The Customer needs to download the blob to a named pipe so they must be able to use a single download connection to prevent seek operations on the pipe

issue_title: Locked cells still copyable
issue_body:
It is still possible to copy and paste locked cells which causes students to insert nbgrader cells with duplicate cell ids - breaking the validation and autograding process. 

As a side note, locked markdown cells can also still be edited with keyboard shortcuts (ex: 1, 2, 3 which alter the markdown header of the first line of the cell) and it isn't easy to revert the cell back to its original state without having to edit the cell metadata.

Some interesting proposals are a button to revert a cell back to its "release" state or to fix some of these edge cases.

issue_title: Allow Multiple ContentsManager
issue_body:
Can we make it possible to allow for multiple content managers in notebook server? 

In a complex environment there may be a more than one type of notebook source so it is better to compose them from the right managers. For example, some notebooks may be from disk, others from a database, while others that purely are read only and exist in memory. Which content manager you call should be driven by the path (`/file/full/path/to/file`, `/db/table/entry`).

I was able to write something that works as a POC for this by taking a ContextManager and then dispatching all calls to a list of internally managed ContextMangers. It simply goes in order of those managers and calls an additional function on each, manages_path(path), which returns true if it wants to manage this path. It then passes on the call to this manager and returns its output. This is crude and would be better if there was a fully supported way to make this happen as I imagine there will likely be similar issues with things like the FileCheckpoints etc.

Would this be something this project would be open to taking up?

issue_title: Notebook should close if permission error happened in loading
issue_body:
When trying to open a notebook with no access permission. Jupyter will throw a dialog alerting: 
"Error loading notebook;   Permission denied:  /path/to/notebook.ipynb"

Then if user clicks "OK", he will stay in a partially opened notebook page, where there is no cell, but menu and toolbar are available to interactive. But most of them will result in errors.

Instead, close this partially opened notebook and return to the file tree view after clicking on "OK" might be better. If user do not have read permission of that notebook.

issue_title: Non scalar values for sigma0?
issue_body:
I am curious as to why only scalar values for sigma0 are allowed. It seems that there shouldnt be any reason why we cannot not have a vector of sigma0 values for each dimension of the optimization problem.

In fact this is possible in the java implementation of CMA-ES: 

https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-math/javadocs/api-3.1/org/apache/commons/math3/optim/nonlinear/scalar/noderiv/CMAESOptimizer.Sigma.html

issue_title: `am.normalize=False` does not take effect
issue_body:
This happens because by default normalize is True and will create the self._norm.
https://github.com/biocommons/hgvs/blob/82c898a11dea70e671415a3d9dcc79b03005051e/hgvs/assemblymapper.py#L70-L71

But in `_maybe_normalize`, it checks `self._norm` rather than `self.normalize`. Thus, even after self.normalize becomes False, self._norm is still not None and projected variants would still be normalized.

https://github.com/biocommons/hgvs/blob/82c898a11dea70e671415a3d9dcc79b03005051e/hgvs/assemblymapper.py#L182-L189

issue_title: Simple use case unsupported
issue_body:
Hello,

Imagine a trivial use case in which we:
 - Read a model from the database
 - Serialize it into JSON
 - Receive updated JSON (from the web)
 - Deserialize it into an SQLAlchemy object
 - Persist the updated object in the database

Lets take a sample model:
```python

class Group(rod.model.db.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'group'

    id = sqlalchemy.schema.Column(sqlalchemy.types.Integer, primary_key=True)
    title = sqlalchemy.schema.Column(sqlalchemy.types.String(100))

    teacher_id = sqlalchemy.schema.Column(sqlalchemy.types.Integer, sqlalchemy.schema.ForeignKey('staff.id'))
    teacher = sqlalchemy.orm.relationship(
        'Staff',  # References the Staff class below
        back_populates='groups'
    )

class Staff(rod.model.db.Model, rod.model.PersistentMixin):
    __tablename__ = 'staff'

    id = sqlalchemy.schema.Column(sqlalchemy.types.Integer, primary_key=True)

    # Personal Information
    name = sqlalchemy.schema.Column(sqlalchemy.types.String)
    groups = sqlalchemy.orm.relationship(
        'Group',
        back_populates='teacher'
    )
```

Since marshmallow-sqlalchemy converts relation fields to IDs between serialization and deserialization, the JSON string `{"teacher": 1}` when deserialized on Group becomes `Group.teacher: Object<Teacher>`, which looks like a new Teacher object, although it may be existing in the database.

The result, when you try to simply save the Group instance, is this:

> FlushError: New instance <Group at 0x1088fd5d0> with identity key (<class 'rod.model.group.Group'>, (26,)) conflicts with persistent instance <Group at 0x1089c74d0>

issue_title: slow WARP evaluation
issue_body:
When evaluating implicit feedbacks (using average mean precision@k for example or even AUC), one needs to predict all users against all items. That is done by looping over the interactions matrix row by row and invoking model.predict()
That gets prohibitively time expensive if faced with a 300K x 20MM matrix. (I estimated ~5days on a 8core server using a simple MF model w/ no content features)
Cython might be able to cut this time by 30%. Sharding the user base and distributing on different servers would reduce that time by another let's say ratio of 10. We would still be looking at 9hours.

What is the way around this? Increasing memory to be able to pass a dense interaction matrix with all ones to model.predict in one or two calls?

issue_title: What is the proper tag for storing an ISBN for audiobooks?
Semantics of Z3_OP_FPA_MIN_I and Z3_OP_FPA_MAX_I plus simplifier bug?
issue_body:
This similar to #1132 . It seems Z3's simplifier is leaking more undocumented operators.

e.g. 

If we start with this

```
; Start decls (3)
(declare-fun r () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
(declare-fun x () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
(declare-fun y () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
; End decls
; Start constraints (4)
(assert (= x (fp #b0 #b00011111000 #xb0db2ac57d287)))
(assert (= y (fp #b1 #b00101101000 #x422c9af77f44c)))
(assert (= r (fp #b0 #b00011111000 #xb0db2ac57d287)))
(assert (= (fp.max x y) r))
; End constraints
```

and then run Z3's simplifier we get

```
; Start decls (3)
(declare-fun r () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
(declare-fun x () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
(declare-fun y () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
; End decls
; Start constraints (4)
(assert (= x (fp #b0 #b00011111000 #xb0db2ac57d287)))
(assert (= y (fp #b1 #b00101101000 #x422c9af77f44c)))
(assert (= r (fp #b0 #b00011111000 #xb0db2ac57d287)))
(assert (let ((a!1 (and (fp.isZero x)
                (fp.isZero y)
                (or (and (fp.isPositive x) (fp.isNegative y))
                    (and (fp.isNegative x) (fp.isPositive y))))))
  (= (ite a!1 (fp.max_unspecified x y) (fp.max_i x y)) r)))
; End constraints
```
`fp.max_i` appears to correspond to `Z3_OP_FPA_MAX_I` kind and `fp.max_unspecified` corresponds publicly to `Z3_OP_UNINTERPRETED` (internally `OP_FPA_INTERNAL_MAX_UNSPECIFIED`)

It would nice if `Z3_OP_FPA_MAX_I` and `Z3_OP_FPA_MIN_I` were documented. It looks like they are introduced by `fpa_rewriter::mk_max()` and `fpa_rewriter::mk_min()`. Reading the implementation I'm guessing the semantics of `Z3_OP_FPA_MAX_I` can be assumed to be that the arguments are not `(+zero)` and `(-zero)` (similar for` Z3_OP_FPA_MAX_I`).

It would be nice if `OP_FPA_INTERNAL_MAX_UNSPECIFIED` (and the others see `Z3_get_decl_kind()` implementation) had their own public separate kinds because I need to handle each of these specially if the simplifier is going to introduce them. `Z3_OP_UNINTERPRETED` isn't enough information for my use case. I could get the string value of the AST but that's kind of a hack.

On a related noted to me it seems like there's a bug (or missing feature) when the `propagate-values` and `simplifer` tactics are combined.

```
; Start decls (3)
(declare-fun r () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
(declare-fun x () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
(declare-fun y () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
; End decls
; Start constraints (4)
(assert (= x (fp #b0 #b00011111000 #xb0db2ac57d287)))
(assert (= y (fp #b1 #b00101101000 #x422c9af77f44c)))
(assert (= r (fp #b0 #b00011111000 #xb0db2ac57d287)))
(assert (= (fp.max x y) r))
; End constraints
(apply (and-then propagate-values simplify) :print_benchmark true :print false)
```

The result is

```
(declare-fun x () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
(declare-fun y () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
(declare-fun r () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
(assert (= x (fp #b0 #b00011111000 #xb0db2ac57d287)))
(assert (= y (fp #b1 #b00101101000 #x422c9af77f44c)))
(assert (= r (fp #b0 #b00011111000 #xb0db2ac57d287)))
(assert (= (fp.max_i (fp #b0 #b00011111000 #xb0db2ac57d287)
                     (fp #b1 #b00101101000 #x422c9af77f44c))
           (fp #b0 #b00011111000 #xb0db2ac57d287)))
(check-sat)
```

The `propagate-values` tactic replaces `fp.max` with `fp.max_i` but the `simplify` tactic does not constant fold `fp.max_i`.

I might try and tackle this missing constant folding myself because this looks relatively easy to fix.

issue_title: The Z3_param_descrs type could do with more documentation and examples

This a minor issue but I thought I'd mention that in the C API

* The ``Z3_param_descrs`` type is not one of the documented types
* There isn't an example of using ``Z3_param_descrs`` in ``examples/c/test_capi.c``. It looks like (based on looking at the Doxygen generated documentation) generally you set the parameters on Z3 objects (e.g. ``Z3_solver`` with ``Z3_solver_set_params()``) with a ``Z3_params`` object but when you want to query what parameters a Z3 object is using (e.g. with ``Z3_solver_get_param_descrs()``) you get back a ``Z3_param_descrs`` rather than a ``Z3_params`` object. Is there a reason that clients don't back a ``Z3_params`` object when requesting the parameters that a Z3 object is using? An illustrative example (that also uses ``Z3_params_validate()``, I'm not quite sure what it's for) would probably help here.

issue_title: Problems importing data with self-referencing many-to-many fields when foreign keys do not already exist
issue_body:
Thanks to [this discussion](https://github.com/django-import-export/django-import-export/issues/318) I was able to get importing foreign keys that do not exist to work for one to many relationships. I'm now trying to do the same with many to many relationships.

For example, I have a Product model, with an m2m relationship to itself to represent products that are competitive with each other. Example data:

> product_name | competes_with
> Product A | Product C
> Product B |
> Product C | Product B, Product D

I need Product C to be created while importing row 1 and related to Product A and Product D to be created with importing row 3 and linked to Product C.

The below will _create_ new values, but won't create a corresponding M2M relationship unless the foreign key value already existed. So, in the above example:

- C will be created, but not related to A
- B will be related to C on importing C
- D will be created, but not related to C

The below also works perfectly fine if the foreign keys are in a different model. It's not clear to me why the behavior is different when importing data with _self-referencing_ many-to-many fields.

```
class M2MCreateWithForeignKey(widgets.ManyToManyWidget):
    def __init__(self, model, separator=', ', field='pk', defaults=None, create=False):
        self.model = model
        self.separator = separator
        self.field = field
        self.defaults = defaults
        self.create = create

    def clean(self, value):
        values = filter(None, value.split(self.separator))

        if self.create:
            for v in values:
                self.model.objects.get_or_create(defaults=self.defaults, **{self.field:v})
            return super(M2MCreateWithForeignKey, self).clean(value)
        else:
            try:
                val = super(M2MCreateWithForeignKey, self).clean(value)
            except ObjectDoesNotExist:
                model_name = self.model.__name__
                error = "Imported data includes non-existant %s(s) (%s) "\
                    "\nThis model does not support creating a new %s when importing data."\
                    % (model_name, value, model_name)
                logger.error(error)
                return None
            else:
                return val
```

issue_title: Как правильно выкусывать имена объектов?
issue_body:
Привет! Например у меня такие строки:

"Скиньте документы по клиенту Рога и Копыта за август"
или
"Скиньте документы по клиенту Рик за август"
или 
"Скиньте акты по клиенту Horns and hooves за август"

С учетом того, что "клиенту" у меня разбирается в ENTITY_G = gram('Entity')
, а "август" разбирается в DATE_G 
```
DATE_G = or_(
    rule(
        DAY.interpretation(Date.day),
        MONTH_NAME.interpretation(Date.month.inflected()),
        YEAR.interpretation(Date.year)
    ),
   ....
).interpretation(
    Date
).named('DATE')
```

Т.е. имена клиентов могут быть и на русском и на английском и на латышском и могут не обрамляться кавычками.

issue_title: expose the font version string generation code for third-party programs to use them

Due to https://github.com/googlefonts/fontbakery/issues/1563 I've been wondering. I can do any of these:

* copy/paste some of your python code into FontBakery
* install font-v as a dependency and invoke it using subprocess.popen
* refactor font-v so that the code that generates version strings can be nicely imported into fontbakery and used

I'd definitely prefer the third option!

So, I see that the bulk of the code is on `app.py` inside the main function. Would it be OK to move some of that functionality into a separate python file (it maybe could even be the utils.py file) and then export that as a python module ?

```python
from fontv.utils import FontVersionObj as fvo
expected = fvo.compute_version_string(fontpath, sha1=True)
```

issue_title: Missing arguments in generated provisioning URI
issue_body:
According to [Key Uri Format](https://github.com/google/google-authenticator/wiki/Key-Uri-Format) there are three more optional arguments: `algorithm`, `digits` and `period`; that if they are set to something different than their defaults: `SHA1`, `6` and `30` respectively; they should be encoded in the generated URI.

Those values are set when the class `TOTP` is instantiated but are not used by the `build_uri` in the `utils.py` module.

issue_title: UnicodeEncodeError in LazyString.__repr__ for python 2
issue_body:
`LazyString.__repr__` calls `str.format` and if the argument is unicode string with non-ascii symbols then it throws `UnicodeEncodeError`.

How to reproduce:
```
In [47]: flask_babel.lazy_gettext(u'qwe').__repr__()
Out[47]: "l'qwe'"

In [48]: flask_babel.lazy_gettext(u'фыв').__repr__()
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
UnicodeEncodeError                        Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-48-174fa9438d66> in <module>()
----> 1 flask_babel.lazy_gettext(u'фыв').__repr__()

/Users/simplylizz/.pyenv/versions/2.7.14/envs/canvas/lib/python2.7/site-packages/flask_babel/speaklater.pyc in __repr__(self)
     18
     19     def __repr__(self):
---> 20         return "l'{0}'".format(text_type(self))
     21
     22     def __str__(self):

UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode characters in position 0-2: ordinal not in range(128)
```

Perhaps you could fix this with by replacing inside `__repr__` `str.format` call on `unicode.format` or just by using `%s`-based formatting which handles such situations without exceptions:
```
In [56]: LazyString.__repr__ = lambda self: "l'%s'" % (flask_babel._compat.text_type(self), )

In [57]: flask_babel.lazy_gettext(u'qwe').__repr__()
Out[57]: u"l'qwe'"

In [58]: flask_babel.lazy_gettext(u'фыв').__repr__()
Out[58]: u"l'\u0444\u044b\u0432'"
```

issue_title: Should `add_*` functions also use `KeyCollection` members rather than literals?
issue_body:
So currently when we do something like say `add_photometry`, we pass a list of `kwargs` to the function where each kwarg is really a string:

```Python
catalog.entries[name].add_photometry(time=mjd, band=band, magnitude=magnitude, 
    e_magnitude=e_magnitude, source=source)
```

But, since say `magnitude` could be changed to a different string later, it might be better to pass the kwargs as a dictionary, with the dictionary members pulling from the `KeyCollection` members, like so:

```Python
catalog.entries[name].add_photometry(**{PHOTOMETRY.TIME: mjd, PHOTOMETRY.BAND=band, 
    PHOTOMETRY.MAGNITUDE=magnitude, PHOTOMETRY.E_MAGNITUDE=e_magnitude, 
    PHOTOMETRY.SOURCE=source})
```

This makes the `add_*` calls a bit longer but also more stable to future changes. Should we adopt this as a best practice?

issue_title: Bindings for void pointers passed by reference
Write-only storages
issue_body:
Just like we have read-only storages, I would like to propose the idea of write-only storages. The idea here is that vdirsyncer would keep the data in a write-only storage up-to-date when synced, but would never pull changes from that storage (and would, in fact, destroy any changes made to that storage locally).

The use case would be a storage which is used to display information, but should never be modified by any tool. By making that storage write-only, one ensures that no accidental modifications to the storage will ever make their way back to the canonical copy of the data and, furthermore, that such accidental modifications will be erased and restored back to the canonical data on the next sync.

issue_title: Add --no-binary flag to pip for packages without wheels
issue_labels: [{'id': 276243379, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20certbot-auto', 'name': 'area: certbot-auto', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 149596829, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/good%20first%20issue', 'name': 'good first issue', 'color': 'c61aff', 'default': True}, {'id': 281347824, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/has%20pr', 'name': 'has pr', 'color': '0052cc', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
`certbot-auto`/`letsencrypt-auto` require specific versions of all our Python dependencies and verifies the hashes of all downloaded packages. Doing this increases both the security and the stability of the scripts. To do this, however, we need to include a hash of each installation mechanism uploaded to PyPI (sdists, wheels, etc.).

Over the weekend, `pycparsing` uploaded a new installation mechanism to PyPI. Previously, they only had a sdist available, but over the weekend they uploaded a wheel. When using `pip` to install packages from PyPI, it prefers wheels over sdists. The result of all of this is when using `certbot-auto` to install `pycparsing`, `pip` would download the wheel, check it against the hash(es) we have for `pycparsing`, and exit with a message about an invalid hash.

`pycparsing` has since removed this wheel due to other problems with it resolving our issue. In general, I think it is uncommon to add a distribution mechanism for an already released version of a package. In my experience, things like this are usually added when a new version of the package is released.

With that said, there's nothing stopping us from having this problem in the future. To try and help mitigate this problem, perhaps we should add `--no-binary` to our usage of `pip` for packages that currently do not have a wheel available. Using this approach, if a wheel is added to one of our dependencies without bumping the package version, `certbot-auto` users are unaffected.

issue_title: Smoketest fails with KeyError if no geth is installed
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

If no `geth` binary is installed (or not on the `PATH`) on the user's system and no `RST_GETH_BINARY` env variable is defined `raiden smoketest` will fail with the following exception:

```
1/5] getting smoketest configuration
[2/5] starting ethereum
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "build/raiden/raiden-script.py", line 2, in <module>
  File "raiden/__main__.py", line 11, in main
  File "click/core.py", line 722, in __call__
  File "click/core.py", line 697, in main
  File "click/core.py", line 1066, in invoke
  File "click/core.py", line 895, in invoke
  File "click/core.py", line 535, in invoke
  File "click/decorators.py", line 17, in new_func
  File "raiden/ui/cli.py", line 829, in smoketest
  File "raiden/tests/utils/smoketest.py", line 315, in start_ethereum
  File "string.py", line 130, in substitute
  File "string.py", line 123, in convert
  File "os.py", line 669, in __getitem__
KeyError: 'RST_GETH_BINARY'
```

The reason is that https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/86d19e6bed94043b0982e26b82264a0e55ab5c97/raiden/tests/utils/smoketest.py#L315 uses `os.environ` as the substitution dict and `DEFAULT_ETH_COMMAND` contains `$RST_GETH_BINARY` which won't be set if `distutils.spawn.find_executable()` returns `None`.

## Solution

Verify that `geth` is available and if it isn't, inform the user with an actionable error message.

## Tasklist

- [ ] Verify geth is available in smoketest
- [ ] Notify user and abort if it isn't
- [ ] Add a changelog entry.

issue_title: Support closing the NettingChannel without requiring a transfer
issue_body:
To close the channel a participant is required to make a transfer, there are only two methods available for closing the smart contract, `closeSingleTransfer` and `close`, the first require one transfer from either party and the later a transfer from each.

- [ ] Implement a close function that doesnt required a transfer for either participant.

issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Apart from noting the address down when starting raiden there is no way for a user to query for their own address while raiden is operational

## Solution

Add a REST API endpoint that would return our raiden address.

## Tasklist

- [ ] Add a REST API endpoint that would return our address. The endpoint URL should be: `/api/<version>/address` and should simply return the ethereum address with which we initialized our raiden node.
- [ ] Document the endpoint in Rest-API.rst

issue_title: Remote functions do not properly handle kwargs.
issue_body:
Currently, we have the following behavior.

```python
import ray

ray.init()

@ray.remote
def f():
  return

f.remote(x=1)  # This does not throw an exception, but it should.
```

issue_title: Actors cannot be created before ray.init is called.
issue_body:
It's important to be able to define remote functions and things like that before a call to `ray.init` (for example, these may be defined in a Python module which are imported at the top of the file before you call `ray.init`).

The same is probably true for actors. We should enable actors to be defined before a call to `ray.init` by caching the created actors and exporting them when `ray.init` is eventually called.

issue_title: GAF Columns that process pipe separated IDs should drop entire line on any one bad ID
issue_body:
The GAF parser parses certain columns that are pipe separated IDs a little differently than other columns. For example, the reference column processes pipe separated PMID IDs: `PMID:12345|PMID:54321`. 

Currently, if any ID is found to be invalid (say because of bad characters in the ID) it is dropped, but the line remains. 

The bug is when there is one ID with an error, the ID is dropped, essentially making columns processed in this way potentially disappear. For example, if the reference column has:
`PMID:1234[2]` this gets converted by the parser into an empty list. 

We should drop the whole line if we see an error in any one pipe separated ID.

issue_title: Add prefix search option for `get_datasets_for_location`
issue_body:
@jeremyh pointed out on slack that `get_datasets_for_location` will be affected by the recent changes to better support stacked datasets (see #415)

Previously we assumed that `dataset.location` points to a file, so reverse query from file to a set of stacked datasets worked by just finding all datasets that have locations with exact match.

https://github.com/opendatacube/datacube-core/blob/5f7211d2eb26ac10d6dfb754418d4bd3079cdf7a/datacube/drivers/postgres/_api.py#L252-L263

Now however every dataset will get a unique location uri, with fragment part (e.g. `file://some/path/file.nc#part=0`, same applies for multi-doc yaml documents too). So to get the original behaviour back we need a version of that same query but with string prefix match instead of equality check. Any tools that uses this will need to be updated to use prefix search option.

Bonus: this would allow querying a folder as well.

issue_title: Give a better error message if someone tries to await a non-trio-flavored awaitable?
issue_body:
This is a very minor issue but:

Right now code like this blows up with a weird `TrioInternalError`:
```python
import asyncio
import trio
async def main():
    await asyncio.sleep(1)
trio.run(main)
```
(NB you can't do `trio.run(asyncio.sleep, 1)` because `asyncio.sleep` is an old-style coroutine function and trio doesn't recognize those.)

It would be nicer if it instead gave a sensible error message.

issue_title: ContentLength not correctly set for Java sdk 2.0 S3Client when getting object
issue_body:
When I perform a get-object request through the CLI I receive a ContentLength in the response:

`aws --endpoint-url=http://localhost:4572 s3api get-object --key tmp/some_file.tar.gz --bucket somebucketname C:\Users\User\Downloads\some_file.tar.gz`
```
{
    "LastModified": "Thu, 01 Mar 2018 08:13:42 GMT",
    "ContentLength": 25,
    "ETag": "\"e1c1a37e204f89a266e6bba6bb26e332\"",
    "ContentType": "binary/octet-stream",
    "Metadata": {}
}
```

However, when performing the same request in code using the Java sdk 2.0 S3Client the ContentLength is null:

```
ResponseInputStream<GetObjectResponse> responseInputStream = s3Client.getObject(GetObjectRequest.builder()
                    .bucket(bucketname)
                    .key(filename)
                    .build());
Long contentLength = responseInputStream.response().contentLength(); //is null
```

The S3Client works correctly and sets the contentLength property correctly in the response when accessing a file in a real bucket in AWS, so I suspect that there is something wrong in LocalStack causing this behaviour.

issue_title: Q: using whitespace as an operator?
issue_body:
I'm trying to write a query language with boolean algebra. It seems to work as I intend, but I'd like to expand it to support whitespace as an implicit AND. For example, `foo bar OR baz` would mean `foo AND bar OR baz`. My attempts always seem to end up in some corner case blowing up spectacularly. My current working baseline grammar is:

```
query_grammar = '''
?sum: product
| sum _or product -> op_or

?product: term
| product _and term -> op_and

?term: query
| _not term -> op_not
| "(" sum ")"

_and: _WHITESPACE "and"i _WHITESPACE

_or: _WHITESPACE "or"i _WHITESPACE

_not: "not"i _WHITESPACE

?query: string -> op_query
| prefix ":" string -> op_prefix_query

?prefix: /[a-zA-Z0-9-]+/

?string: quoted_string | plain_string

?quoted_string: ESCAPED_STRING

?plain_string: /[a-zA-Z0-9.@_-]+/

%import common.ESCAPED_STRING
%import common.WS_INLINE -> _WHITESPACE
'''
```

Any suggestions?
